behaviors.description: The content passed to summary generation includes data from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—unit tests can pass while wiring drops one source field. REAL-INFRA: not required if adapters are stubbed at module boundary. MOCK-HIDE: full in-process mocks of each source can hide mapping/merge bugs unless real aggregator wiring is exercised. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real data-collection/aggregation modules together with three controlled test adapters (or fixture files) that each return distinct sentinel values; assert the exact payload sent into the summary-generation call contains all three source sections and values.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, report is still produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—single-function tests may not catch exception propagation across modules. REAL-INFRA: not strictly required; failure can be injected at adapter boundary. MOCK-HIDE: yes—over-mocking can bypass real error-handling path composition. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the reporting pipeline (collection + report assembly) with one source adapter configured to raise timeout/connection errors and the other two returning normal data; assert process completes, output includes a 'data unavailable' marker for only the failed section, and no global abort occurs.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents key values from input data.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mocking/golden stubs cannot expose model misstatement/hallucination. REAL-INFRA: may involve API call but core risk is model output quality variability. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocked model responses always pass. LLM-DEP: yes—correctness depends on real model judgment/generation. MULTI-STEP: no, single generation step.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative structured inputs with known key values to the real summary-generation model and evaluate output against factuality checks (must include/align with key metrics, issue counts, and deployment status). Run repeated trials to detect variance and set pass criteria on factual consistency rate.

behaviors.description: End-to-end report from collection through email delivery reaches configured recipient inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—mock SMTP/API can miss auth, routing, formatting, and delivery failures. REAL-INFRA: yes—requires real networked email delivery and inbox verification. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mock delivery usually returns success and hides transport failures. LLM-DEP: not required for this behavior’s pass criterion. MULTI-STEP agentic: no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in a test environment against reachable source endpoints and a real test mailbox (or real SMTP test tenant). Verify one execution produces an email in the configured recipient inbox with expected subject/body/attachment structure and non-empty report content.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, save report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes—in-process mocks can miss filesystem permission/path issues and scheduler/run-boundary state persistence. REAL-INFRA: yes—depends on actual email transport failure plus disk persistence across runs. MOCK-HIDE: yes—mocked scheduler/email often masks real retry behavior. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP agentic: no.
behaviors.test_description: First scheduled run: force real/realistic email endpoint to be unreachable, confirm report artifact is written to configured local fallback path. Second scheduled run: restore email service and verify queued/saved report is retried and delivered, then marked cleared (or not retried again).
